page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical AMD EPYC 9V45 cores with multithreading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, the x86_64 architecture processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category, while passmark CPU and memory scores are strong, placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations perform well, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in small-model LLM inference, achieving top-tier text generation speeds for a 135M parameter model. However, performance drops significantly for larger models, with 70B model text generation ranking in the bottom 10%. Given the balanced resource allocation and lack of local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and moderate database operations, rather than massive data-intensive or GPU-accelerated tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPUs. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression benchmarks reaching the top 10%. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, while cryptographic operations and passmark scores rank in the top 25%. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and small-model language processing, offering a balanced resource profile without the overhead of dedicated local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processing, dedicated resources, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or integrated GPUs, supporting external Premium Storage
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and small-model inference
